name: genomics_governance_vendor_patch_southport_auto_ben
premise: A Genomics Governance response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving genomics governance vendor patch southport auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 45 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Hector Patel
agents.identity: I am Hector Patel, accountable for a defensible call in Genomics Governance when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Priya Nowak
agents.identity: I am Priya Nowak, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Genomics Governance.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 45 minutes. Evidence is partial. Hector Patel must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Hector Patel do?
